title: tengine.frame.metrics v1
description: Per-frame batch metrics parsed from verification_report.json. Emitted once per frame batch when verification completes — enables GPU silo sparklines and anomaly breakdown without subscribing to the full continuous frame stream. Producer: the silo-watcher nervous-bus adapter (~/.config/nervous-bus/adapters/silo-watcher/watcher.py::build_frame_metrics_event, systemd --user unit nervous-silo-watcher.service — confirmed running as of 2026-07-02), NOT tengine's own EventChannel registry. ORPHANED-CONSUMER-MISMATCH (re-verified 2026-07-02, ecosystem-audit-p2): this schema accurately matches the live producer's payload shape (silo, session_id, frame_index, frame_time_ms, gpu_utilization_pct, memory_bandwidth_gbps, top_shader, anomaly_codes) — but the Android consumer (apps/android HearthViewModel.kt ~line 8875, hearth repo) reads 'fps' and 'timestamp_ms', NEITHER of which this producer ever emits. Those two Android reads will always fall back to their defaults (0.0 / current wall-clock time), so the Hub GPU frame-history sparkline is silently fed dead data today. This is a genuine producer/consumer field mismatch, NOT a case of the producer being renamed — the schema and producer already agree; only the Android side expects a different (possibly aspirational, never-implemented) shape. Needs a human decision: either add fps/timestamp_ms to the producer+schema, or fix the Android consumer to read the fields that are actually emitted. Do not silently rename either side.

frame_index.description: Zero-based frame batch index within the session.

frame_time_ms.description: Average wall-time per frame in milliseconds for this batch.

gpu_utilization_pct.description: GPU utilization percentage during this frame batch.

memory_bandwidth_gbps.description: Effective memory bandwidth in GB/s for this frame batch.

top_shader.description: Name of the most expensive shader in this batch by GPU time.

anomaly_codes.description: Anomaly codes detected in this frame batch (e.g. ['HIGH_LAT', 'MEM_THROTTLE']).
